title: Common Faults — MacBook Pro 13" M1 (A2338, 820-02020)
WARNING: A2338 is MBP 13" M1. Not MBA. Has Touch Bar, fan, 2x USB-C, no MagSafe.

faults.symptom: No power at all — no LED, no fan spin, no PPBUS_AON
faults.likely_cause: Charger circuit failure or battery disconnect
diagnosis: Check FF201 fuse (page 55) — blown fuse = short downstream
Check J5150 solder pads (page 21) — BMU flex is SOLDERED, can crack
Check SYS_DETECT circuit (Q5155, S5190/S5191)
Check U5200 charger IC (353S01525) for shorts
Check PPOCIN_USBC_AON for voltage when charger connected

faults.likely_cause: U5700 SVR AON failure or enable signal not reaching it
diagnosis: Check P3V8AON_PWR_EN — is it asserted? (delayed by U5340 circuit, page 23)
Check P3V8AON_FAULT_L — if LOW, fault condition in U5700
Check for short on PP3V8_AON rail — thermal image the board
Common: short on PP3V8_AON VDDMAIN distribution (page 100)
Check phase driver MOSFETs (Q5800 area, page 26)

faults.likely_cause: UC300 (LT8642S) failure or enable signal missing
diagnosis: Check P5VS2_PWR_EN enable signal
Check UC300 input (PPBUS_AON should be present)
Check for short on PP5V_S2 — diode mode test on output caps
Check 5VS2 discharge circuit: QC420 MOSFET, RC423/RC422 bleeders (page 38)
Check 5VS2_EN turn off delay circuit: DC401, RC401/RC402 (page 38)

faults.symptom: No display but board appears to boot (fan spins, SSD activity)
faults.likely_cause: Display power sequencer, BEN controller, or display connector fault
diagnosis: Check LCD_PWR_EN signal at UP700 (SLG4AP4365V, page 69)
Check PMU_SYS_ALIVE at UP700 pin 3
Check PP5V_SW_LCD and PP3V3_SW_LCD from UP701
Check PPVOUT_LCDBKLT from UP800 BEN (page 70)
Check JP600 display connector for damage/corrosion (page 68)
Check display flex cable seating

faults.likely_cause: DFR power rail failure or DFR connector issue
diagnosis: Check DER_PWR_EN signal
Check PP1V8_AWAKE_DFR from UT700 (page 86/87)
Check PP3V3_AWAKE_DFR from UT710
Check DFR touch connector JT700 (page 86)
Check DFR display connector JT710 (page 86)
Check SPI_DFR signals and level shifters (UT820/UT830/UT860/UT880/UT890)
Check PP5V_S2_TO_FAN at JE501 connector (page 52)
Check QE500 (NTS4C569N) MOSFET — drives fan via SMC_FAN_LS_PWM
Check UE501 level shifter (SN74A4C1T45)
Check fan connector JE501 for damage
Check FAN_RT_TACH signal — if fan connected but no tach, motor may be seized
Check thermal sensor connections (TDEV pages 49-50)

faults.symptom: USB-C port not working (one port only)
faults.likely_cause: CD3217 or retimer failure on affected port
diagnosis: Identify which port: ATC0 = left rear (debug), ATC1 = left front
Check specific CD3217: UF400 (ATC0) or UF500 (ATC1)
Check retimer: UF000 (ATC0) or UF100 (ATC1) on pages 53-54
Check port-specific power: PP3V3_S2_UPC, PP1V25_S2 at that port
Check CC lines for shorts (USBC0_CC1/CC2 or USBC1_CC1/CC2)
Check USB2 chokes: LF800 (ATC0) or LF810 (ATC1) on page 61

faults.likely_cause: Rasputin module or antenna connection failure
diagnosis: Check WLBT_PWR_EN signal
Check PP1V25_S2 and PP3V3_S2 at Rasputin module (page 62)
Check PP3V3_S2_WLBT_ISNS, PP1V8_S2_WLBT_ISNS rails
Check antenna connectors JL110-JL133 (SUNWAY, page 63)
Check PCIE WLAN/BT reset and clock signals
Rasputin module (LBRECAUV1-506) is soldered — rework requires BGA skills
Check PPBUS_SPKRAMP_LEFT_ISNS and PPBUS_SPKRAMP_RIGHT_ISNS (page 78)
Check PP1V25_AWAKE_IO and PP1V8_AWAKE at amp ICs (pages 76-77)
Check UR500 (CS42L83) audio codec (page 75)
Check speaker amp connectors JR800 (left) and JR850 (right) (page 78)
4 amps: A/B/C left (UR600 area), D/E/F right (UR700 area)
Check SPKRAMP_LVS_RESET_L signals

faults.likely_cause: Keyboard IOX, signal connector, or power fault
diagnosis: Check PP3V3_AON_KBD_CONN rail
Check PP1V85_S2_KBD_ISNS (keyboard 1.85V power sense)
Check keyboard IOX1 (UT101, PCA6416A) and IOX2 (UT102) on page 81
Check UT103 (BUMQU03V) RSLOC isolation IC
Check JT200 membrane ZIF connector (30-pin, page 82)
Check keyboard ESD protection (DZT201-DZT206, page 82)

faults.likely_cause: NAND power rail failure or NAND controller fault
diagnosis: Check PP2V5_AWAKE_NAND (SPMU BUCK 6, page 28/100)
Check PP1V25_AWAKE5W_VCCQ (SPMU BUCK 13 SW5)
Check PP0V88_AWAKE5W_NAND (SPMU BUCK 12 SW6)
Check PPVDS_MAME5W_NAND (SPMU BUCK 12 SW7)
Check NAND0 reset: NAND0_RESET_L, NAND0_PCIE_RESET_L (page 67)
Check PMU_SYS_ALIVE → NAND0_PEN_L path (RP002, page 67)
Check non-Ocarina VCC discharge circuit (QN420, page 66)
NAND is soldered — not user-replaceable

faults.likely_cause: Trackpad power, SPI bus, or connector fault
diagnosis: Check PP5V_S2_TPAD_ISNS (via LT400 ferrite, page 84)
Check PP3V3_S2_TPAD (UT305 LDO, page 83)
Check PP1V85_S2_IPD (UT300 LDO, page 83)
Check IPD_PWR_EN signal (AND gate on page 83)
Check JT400 trackpad connector (DF40C-50DS, page 84)
Check SPI TPAD signals and level shifter UT360 (page 83)

faults.likely_cause: Corrosion on critical power or signal paths
diagnosis: Clean board with isopropyl alcohol and ultrasonic cleaner
Inspect all connector areas under microscope
Check for corroded 0201 resistors near PMU (pages 28-35)
Check for corroded level shifter ICs (many SN74AXC1T45 on board)
Check PP3V8_AON distribution — corrosion can cause partial shorts
After cleaning: run full power-on sequence diagnosis from Step 1
